titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.37

p.seg: William by divine permission humble servant of the church of St Andrews to his well beloved Ralph, dean of Liston, and Master Richard of Dover, rector of the church of Tyninghame, and the lord John of Pencaitland, greeting.

We command you that on the vigil of Saint Matthew the apostle, at Haddington, you undertake an assessment of the vicarage of Haddington by our authority to the value of forty marks. And if our steward cannot be present on the said day and place to do this with you, I shall send you on the aforenamed day the lord Robert of Methven, our chaplain, to act as the said steward’s deputy. By no means fail to carry out this our mandate.
